title: quercus × capeloana
intro: Quercus × capeloana, a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Quercus faginea and Quercus robur, is a tree species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Quercus × capeloana include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, lower cholesterol levels, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea or decoction, often combined with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
